content: 2026-06-10 — Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup. Quick eval of terminal multiplexers and my endless tmux config yak-shave. Current situation: my tmux config is ~1,200 lines, uses tpm + tmux-resurrect + tmux-continuum, lots of ad-hoc bindings. Problems: maintenance time (≈3 h/week), slow to tweak, startup feel clunky, plugins sometimes break on older servers. Requirements, in order: 1) must be attachable from minimal Debian/Ubuntu servers (can't install Rust/WASM everywhere), 2) session persistence and easy restore, 3) templated project layouts (I want layouts-as-code), 4) programmatic control from Python/CLI, 5) low config maintenance, ideally <30 min/week. Candidates on my radar: tmux (stay + slim), screen/byobu (compatibility), zellij (modern, layouts YAML, WASM plugins), wezterm mux (local), and using terminal emulator tabs instead. Quick comparison and a decision path please — keep compatibility with 5 production VMs and 2 coworkers who use tmux in mind.

Clear requirements. Quick question: is ability to attach from those 5 production VMs without installing anything new (only apt-get allowed) an absolute constraint, or can you ask ops to install a single new binary there?

Absolute constraint for production: I can ask ops for changes but they’re slow and I don’t want to depend on them. So fallback must be tmux/screen available via apt. Preference order reiterated: 1) compatibility on prod, 2) scripting/layouts, 3) simpler local UX. I’d accept a layered approach: keep minimal tmux on prod, use something nicer locally. Also need teammates to be able to attach to shared sessions occasionally.

Makes sense. Trade-off summary: keep tmux on prod for ubiquity; use modern local tool for ergonomics. Do you prefer a) one config that works everywhere (minimal) or b) split configs: tiny portable tmuxrc on servers + richer local configs (zellij locally)?

I lean split. Plan in my head: extract a minimal tmuxrc (~50 lines) for servers with essential bindings, per-user sockets, minimal status, and leave full 1,200-line config on my laptop for personal comfort. Locally use zellij for project layouts (YAML), because I like pane presets and mouse support. Pros: no ops changes, teammates can still attach to server sessions. Cons: need to learn zellij and write layout YAMLs; install zellij on my laptop. Rough time: 6 h total — 2 h to extract tmuxrc and test on two servers, 3 h to create zellij layouts for my ML project (ipython, logs tail, editor, git), 1 h to write bootstrap and README for team. Also drop tmux-resurrect in favor of simple restore scripts for tmux sockets. Does that sound reasonable?

Yes — reasonable and low-risk. One sharp question: do you want per-user sockets by default (avoid collisions) with an explicit shared socket option for pair sessions, or keep the classic system-wide socket so teammates can attach with no extra steps?

Per-user sockets by default: place sockets under ~/.tmux/sockets to avoid collisions. Add a simple env/flag to create a shared socket under /tmp/tmux-shared-<project> for pairing. Also set default-terminal to tmux-256color but fallback to xterm if on older servers. I'll schedule the work this week.
Good plan. Which day/time will you block to start the first chunk (tmuxrc extraction + server testing)?

I'll block Wednesday 2026-06-16 at 14:00 CET for the tmuxrc extraction and testing on two servers, and Saturday 2026-06-20 at 09:00 CET for zellij layouts and bootstrap scripting. Final decisions: layered approach, ~7 h total, minimal tmuxrc ≈50 lines in ~/.tmux/minimal.conf, per-user sockets in ~/.tmux/sockets, zellij for local layouts, README update for team. I'll replace tmux-resurrect with small restore scripts.
All set. Quick last check: do you want me to generate the 50-line minimal tmuxrc template and the zellij YAML layout now, or do that after your Wednesday 2026-06-16 session?
